parts.prompt: Explain how Soundwave amplitude relates to the scenario.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Response must indicate that relevant to the scenario, a loud sound is associated with high soundwaves, or a soft sound is associated with low soundwaves.
Acceptable: The height of the soundwaves that were created when the lamp crashed must have been; The sound of the lamp crashing was so loud because it had a high amplitude.

parts.prompt: Explain how Procedural memory relates to the scenario.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Response must indicate that Steve and/or the children remember how to physically do something in the scenario (e.g., making breakfast) without paying conscious attention (e.g., automatically, unconsciously, muscle memory, nondeclarative, well-practiced/learned) to doing so.
Acceptable: The children have set the breakfast table so much that they do it automatically.

parts.prompt: Explain how Diffusion of responsibility relates to the scenario.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Response must indicate that the children do not attempt to clean up the broken lamp because they assume others are likely to do it.
Acceptable: The children are not cleaning up the lamp because they think someone else will do it.; The younger children assume the older children will clean up the lamp.

parts.prompt: Explain how Dispositional attribution relates to the scenario.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Response must indicate that Steve and/or the children overestimated the internal factors influencing their own or someone else’s behavior in the scenario.
Acceptable: Steve decided that his children were clumsy and irresponsible.; Steve concludes that he is a mean person for disciplining his children.

parts.prompt: Explain how Ghrelin relates to the scenario.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Response must indicate that for someone in the scenario, ghrelin levels are high when they are hungry or low when they are not hungry.
Acceptable: While the children waited for breakfast, ghrelin caused feelings of hunger.; Steve was able to lower his ghrelin levels because he ate all the breakfast.

parts.prompt: Instead of asking the children what happened, Steve immediately turns off the television and sends them all outside to rake the large amount of leaves in the backyard. Steve stays irritable for the rest of the day. Explain how Authoritarian parenting style relates to the scenario.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Response must indicate that Steve is demonstrating a parenting style that includes high control, no discussion of expectations or consequences (one-way communication), or that focuses on obedience and discipline.
Acceptable: Steve’s parenting style most closely fits authoritarian because he was quick to; Steve was more interested in them following rules than understanding what

parts.prompt: Instead of asking the children what happened, Steve immediately turns off the television and sends them all outside to rake the large amount of leaves in the backyard. Steve stays irritable for the rest of the day. Explain how Steve’s high level of the Big Five trait of neuroticism relates to the scenario.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Response must indicate that an enduring characteristic of neuroticism (e.g., depression, negative feelings, anxiety) explains Steve’s behavior in the scenario.
Acceptable: Steve remains irritable all day and doesn’t seem to recover from this minor event,; Steve experiences an extreme, negative emotional change throughout the scenario,
